source_old_req: The diagnosisEngine thread shall execute periodically with a period of 3000 ms and a deadline of 500 ms, with a priority of 7. The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall call the subprogram diagnosisEngine_spg from the subprograms package. The diagnosisEngine thread shall process each input from the diagnosis_in event data port, which is of type SHM_DataType::errorData, and produce corresponding output to the diagnosis_out event data port, also of type SHM_DataType::errorData. The diagnosisEngine thread shall ensure that the values of diagnosis_out are determined solely by the current input from diagnosis_in and the internal computations of the thread. The diagnosisEngine thread shall maintain the integrity of the data types of diagnosis_in and diagnosis_out, ensuring that no invalid data is propagated through these ports. The diagnosisEngine.impl shall connect the diagnosis_in port to the inData_para parameter of the diagnosisEngine_spg subprogram and shall connect the outData_para parameter of the diagnosisEngine_spg subprogram to the diagnosis_out port. The diagnosisEngine thread shall operate within its specified compute execution time range of 400 ms to 500 ms.
natural_requirement: The diagnosisEngine component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for diagnosisEngine.impl. In the implementation layer, diagnosisEngine.impl shall route data flow from diagnosis_in to diagnosisEngine.inData_para through C1 and data flow from diagnosisEngine.outData_para to diagnosis_out through C2.

requirement_items.text: The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall route the data flow from diagnosis_in to diagnosisEngine.inData_para through connection C1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from diagnosis_in to diagnosisEngine.inData_para.

requirement_items.text: The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall route the data flow from diagnosisEngine.outData_para to diagnosis_out through connection C2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from diagnosisEngine.outData_para to diagnosis_out.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from diagnosis_in to diagnosisEngine.inData_para. The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from diagnosisEngine.outData_para to diagnosis_out.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall route the data flow from diagnosis_in to diagnosisEngine.inData_para through connection C1. The diagnosisEngine.impl implementation shall route the data flow from diagnosisEngine.outData_para to diagnosis_out through connection C2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
